title: Talklines/Warmlines * Perinatal Depression - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Talklines/Warmlines * Perinatal Depression 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Hepatitis Perinatal Depression Reproductive Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BABY BLUES CONNECTION PREGNANCY AND POSTPARTUM SUPPORT RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support for parents and birthing people coping with perinatal mood and anxiety disorders through connection, information and resources. Services include 24-hour phone support, parent to parent support groups online and in-person and partner support for family members who want to support someone coping with postpartum depression or anxiety. Resource packets may be emailed or mailed at no cost. PO Box 33128 Portland, OR 97292 Eligibility: Parents experiencing any kind of perinatal mood disorders and stress related to welcoming a new child, and their family members. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 557-8375 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POSTPARTUM SUPPORT INTERNATIONAL POSTPARTUM SUPPORT INTERNATIONAL HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential helpline that offers support and local referrals. Shares online information and support groups focused on the emotional and mental health of childbearing families. The Postpartum Support International website helps users locate specialized support coordinators, crisis hotlines, support groups, and local events by state or county. 6706 SW 54th Avenue Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Pregnant and postpartum individuals, parents, and family members Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 944-4773 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WELLMAMA MATERNAL MENTAL HEALTH AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, support groups, and wellness services to people affected by or at risk for mental health conditions related to parenthood. Support people, including partners and babies under age 1, are welcome at all meetings. 576 Olive Street Suite 205 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Families struggling with emotions related to reproductive health, such as pregnancy, the first few years postpartum, and perinatal loss Hours: Support group times vary Email (541) 525-0495 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BABY BLUES CONNECTION PREGNANCY AND POSTPARTUM SUPPORT RESOURCES POSTPARTUM SUPPORT INTERNATIONAL POSTPARTUM SUPPORT INTERNATIONAL HELPLINE WELLMAMA MATERNAL MENTAL HEALTH AND SUPPORT SERVICES
